title: [phase:critique] [family:ui-runtime-review] [provider_hint:subagent] Review the current Happy production resolution. Facts: production /happy/ serves updated HTML with Live Session wired to /cockpit; /cockpit returns 200; unauthenticated GET /api/happy/state,/events,/task-detail return 401 by design...
summary_text: 1. **Refresh replay/race (best minimal fix):** make rotation consume the old refresh token exactly once, and only mint new token if consume succeeded. `/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts` - Change `revokeRefreshToken` to return `boolean` from `run().meta.changes === 1`. - In `rotateRefreshToken`, keep current `verify + is_active` checks, then `const consumed = await revokeRefreshToken(oldToken, env); if (!consumed) return null;` before issuing new token. - This is enough to close concurrent replay without schema changes. 2. **D1 lookup failure vs inactive/not-found (best minimal fix):** return a distinguishable auth-backend failure signal and map to `503`, not `401`. `/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts`, `/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts`, `/src/durable-objects/cockpit-websocket.ts` - Introduce a small typed result for auth-state lookup (or at minimum a distinct error code), e.g. `AUTH_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE`. - `authenticateRequest`: - inactive/not-found => `401` behavior unchanged - D1 unavailable/query failure => `503` + stable error code - WebSocket upgrade should apply the same distinction for `getUserAuthState` failure (return `503` instead of “inactive/unauthorized”). 3. **Verification (small but required):** add tests for - concurrent/second rotation attempt returns null and does not mint token, - D1 lookup failure yields backend-unavailable code/status (HTTP + WS path). **Blocker:** no hard blocker. Only integration caution: clients that currently treat all auth failures as “re-login” must handle `503 AUTH_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE` as retry/maintenance, not logout.
